_risk_level_doc: high: change is in hook authorization/cleanup infrastructure; security invariant (single-use grant semantics) depends on correct behavior

requirement.original: Fix the legacy grant subagent leak in the /allow hook infrastructure.
requirement.clarified: Remove the subagent-context guard in posttool-allowlist-consume.py so that legacy grant files are always unlinked on terminal result, regardless of whether execution context is subagent or main-agent. Also remove the now-dead is_subagent_context import and update stale docstrings.
requirement.what: Remove `if not is_subagent_context(data):` guard at posttool-allowlist-consume.py:88; remove dead import; update docstrings
requirement.why: When a subagent executes an /allow-authorized Bash command, the sentinel is consumed but the legacy grant file is left on disk, allowing the main agent to reuse it for a second command invocation — violating single-use grant semantics

included: Remove dead import from lib.subagent import is_subagent_context at line 32

success_criteria: Legacy grant file is unlinked after any subagent-executed Bash PostToolUse terminal result
Main-agent legacy grant cleanup behavior is unchanged (regression)
Write firewall in userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh Step 0 is unchanged
No double-consume panic when legacy grant is absent
constraints: consume_grant_for_posttool() already handles FileNotFoundError silently (hooks/lib/allowlist.py:333) — no additional error handling needed
The fix must not widen PreToolUse subagent authorization (legacy grant read path in pretool-bash-safety.sh remains gated by IS_SUBAGENT)

path_dependent_shared_infra: hooks/lib/allowlist.py (consume_grant_for_posttool — read to confirm FileNotFoundError handling; no code change)
hooks/lib/subagent.py (is_subagent_context — import being removed; no code change)

root_cause_analysis.symptom: When a subagent executes an /allow-authorized Bash command, the legacy grant file /tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-<sid>.json remains on disk after the sentinel is consumed.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: posttool-allowlist-consume.py:87-89 guards consume_grant_for_posttool() behind `if not is_subagent_context(data)`. The comment says 'subagents use sentinel path' — this was accurate when subagents could not use sentinel grants, but since task 20260521-090200 (M2) extended sentinel grants to subagents, the guard now produces an asymmetric cleanup: sentinel consumed, legacy not consumed.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — no single commit; design gap introduced when M2 extended sentinel grants to subagents without updating posttool cleanup symmetry
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: The original intent of the guard was correct: before M2, subagents could not use legacy grants, so no cleanup was needed. After M2, sentinel grants began authorizing subagent Bash commands, but the posttool hook was not updated to match.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Both the legacy and sentinel grant are written by /allow simultaneously. After a subagent-authorized execution, the sentinel is consumed but the legacy grant remains. The main agent can then read and consume the legacy grant for a second, unauthorized Bash command execution.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Gap created by task 20260521-090200 (M2 — sentinel grants extended to subagents) without a corresponding update to posttool-allowlist-consume.py
observations: posttool-allowlist-consume.py:87-89: guard `if not is_subagent_context(data):` skips consume_grant_for_posttool() for subagent payloads (inferred: source read)
hooks/lib/allowlist.py:333: `except FileNotFoundError: return False` confirms absent-file is handled silently (inferred: source read)
userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh:155,181-242: both legacy and sentinel files are written at the same time (inferred: source read)
pretool-bash-safety.sh:145: IS_SUBAGENT guard in check_and_consume_allowlist blocks subagents from using legacy grant at PreTool time (inferred: source read)
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: incomplete (inferred only — no runtime observation performed; root cause is unambiguous from source code)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: Source code read at posttool-allowlist-consume.py:87-89 directly shows the guard. No runtime observation needed — the code path is deterministic.

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: not a regression complaint — new gap identified by user analysis

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: the legacy grant file is left on disk
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: After subagent executes an /allow-authorized Bash command, legacy grant file is absent

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: The main agent can now match and reuse the legacy grant for a second command invocation, bypassing single-use semantics
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: Main agent cannot consume an already-subagent-executed grant (because it is unlinked by PostToolUse)

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: Legacy grant must be consumed once, regardless of whether main agent or subagent triggered consumption
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: consume_grant_for_posttool() is called unconditionally on any Bash PostToolUse event

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: Subagents MUST NOT write new grants (write firewall — unchanged)
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh Step 0 still exits early when agent_id present

component_chain.role: Writes both legacy grant and sentinel grant when /allow is invoked by user
At PreToolUse: reads legacy grant for main-agent Bash authorization (IS_SUBAGENT=1 blocks subagents from this path); reads sentinel grant for subagent Bash authorization (lines 496-538)
component_chain.evidence: Read: lines 145 (IS_SUBAGENT firewall in check_and_consume_allowlist) and 482-538 (sentinel block extended to subagents per M2)

component_chain.role: At PostToolUse: consumes legacy grant (currently guarded by is_subagent_context) and sentinel grant. THE BUG IS HERE.
consume_grant_for_posttool(): atomic match+unlink of legacy grant under flock; silently returns False on FileNotFoundError

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet:
if not is_subagent_context(data):
    consume_grant_for_posttool(session_id, tool_name, command)
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Prevents legacy grant cleanup for subagent context — originally correct, now causes leak
Prevents subagents from using legacy grant path at PreToolUse time — MUST remain unchanged
Write firewall — prevents subagents from writing any /allow grant — MUST remain unchanged

setup.reason: non-UI -- hook; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths

evidence.observed: When a subagent executes a Bash command authorized by a sentinel grant, the legacy grant file at /tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-<sid>.json is left on disk and can be reused by the main agent for a second command.

measured.value: if not is_subagent_context(data): consume_grant_for_posttool(session_id, tool_name, command)

expected.value: consume_grant_for_posttool(session_id, tool_name, command) — unconditional, no subagent guard
expected.source: Security invariant: both grant files written together must be cleaned up together; single-use semantics apply regardless of execution context

evidence.gap: Legacy grant survives subagent-executed Bash PostToolUse events; sentinel is cleaned up but legacy is not

additional_found: hooks/lib/subagent.py (imported symbol — no code change needed)

reference_source.description: Source code read directly in this analysis session

context.recent_commits: aea75d9 chore(docs): ship cycle 20260522-000000 close-report and QA verification

development_approach.strategy: Single-file surgical fix: remove the is_subagent_context guard and dead import from posttool-allowlist-consume.py; update stale docstrings. No other files need to change.
development_approach.validation_approach: Subprocess tests invoking posttool-allowlist-consume.py directly with crafted stdin payloads (subagent and main-agent variants); verify file presence/absence of both grant types after hook runs.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised 4 blockers and 3 issues. All blockers applied: (1) AC3 now cites correct write firewall location (userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh:17-23, not pretool-bash-safety.sh:145); (2) ACs 1/2 rewritten as hook-entrypoint subprocess tests; (3) dead is_subagent_context import added as explicit deletion requirement; (4) AC6 added to prove PreTool subagent authorization remains blocked for legacy-only grants. Issues 5/6/7 incorporated: AC4 specifies both exit_code=0 and non-zero cases; AC5 tests fail-open hook behavior not just library FileNotFoundError; complexity note updated to SMALL/security-sensitive.

findings.issue: AC3 cites pretool-bash-safety.sh:145 as the write firewall, but the actual write firewall is userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh Step 0 (lines 17-23)
findings.proposed_fix: Change AC3 note to cite userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh Step 0
findings.rationale: Incorrect citation would cause dev/QA to verify the wrong file

findings.issue: ACs should test posttool-allowlist-consume.py as an entrypoint (subprocess), not consume_grant_for_posttool() in isolation
findings.proposed_fix: Rewrite AC1/AC2 as subprocess tests with stdin payloads
findings.rationale: The bug is in the caller guard, not the library function; testing the library in isolation would pass before and after the fix

findings.issue: Removing the guard makes is_subagent_context import dead code — must be removed too
findings.proposed_fix: Add explicit requirement to remove the import at line 32
findings.rationale: Dead import triggers lint warnings and is misleading

findings.issue: No AC verifying that the fix does not widen PreTool subagent authorization for legacy-only grants
findings.proposed_fix: Add AC6: legacy-only subagent PreToolUse still blocked by pretool-bash-safety.sh IS_SUBAGENT firewall
findings.rationale: Critical security regression check — fix must only affect PostTool cleanup, not PreTool authorization

findings.issue: AC4 too vague about sentinel consumption — should specify both exit_code=0 and non-zero
findings.proposed_fix: AC4 now specifies both terminal result cases
findings.rationale: Consume-on-any-terminal-result is the contract; both cases should be tested

findings.issue: AC5 should assert hook fail-open behavior (exit 0, no traceback), not just FileNotFoundError in helper
findings.proposed_fix: AC5 now checks hook exit_code=0 and absence of traceback in stderr
findings.rationale: The security property is hook-level fail-open, not just library-level exception handling

findings.issue: SMALL complexity is acceptable but implementation is security-sensitive
findings.rationale: risk_level already set to high; no spec change needed beyond the note

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 4 blockers applied; 2 major issues applied; 1 observation logged
